messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":80,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"New York","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"bachelor's degree","parent_2":"master's degree"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"curious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["reading novels","hiking","painting"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":3,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":21,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":68,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":14,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":7,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

  "opinion": "As an 80-year-old retired man living in New York, I've seen a lot in my time, from polio scares to the AIDS crisis, and now COVID-19. Managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines, in my view, boils down to a mix of personal experience, faith, and a straightforward approach, given my low agreeableness and high extraversion. I'm not one to sugarcoat things or shy away from a debate, especially after spending hours daily on social media where these topics flare up. My Christian beliefs guide me to value life and community, so I support vaccines as a way to protect others, much like how we'd help a neighbor in need. However, with low openness, I tend to stick to what I know works from past health crises, trusting doctors and public health advice over newfangled theories. I've raised two kids and been married for decades, so I understand family concerns, but I don't have patience for endless arguments—I'd rather share my own stories, like how getting vaccinated let me keep hiking and painting without fear. In discussions, I focus on practical outcomes: vaccines keep hospitals from overflowing and let us gather safely, which matters in a bustling city like New York. My curiosity drives me to ask questions, but I rely on trusted sources, not internet rumors. Ultimately, I believe in speaking my mind clearly, respecting others' choices but standing firm on science and faith, without getting overly emotional, thanks to my low neuroticism.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As an 80-year-old retired man living in New York, I've seen a lot in my time, from polio scares to the AIDS crisis, and now COVID-19. Managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines, in my view, boils down to a mix of personal experience, faith, and a straightforward approach, given my low agreeableness and high extraversion. I'm not one to sugarcoat things or shy away from a debate, especially after spending hours daily on social media where these topics flare up. My Christian beliefs guide me to value life and community, so I support vaccines as a way to protect others, much like how we'd help a neighbor in need. However, with low openness, I tend to stick to what I know works from past health crises, trusting doctors and public health advice over newfangled theories. I've raised two kids and been married for decades, so I understand family concerns, but I don't have patience for endless arguments—I'd rather share my own stories, like how getting vaccinated let me keep hiking and painting without fear. In discussions, I focus on practical outcomes: vaccines keep hospitals from overflowing and let us gather safely, which matters in a bustling city like New York. My curiosity drives me to ask questions, but I rely on trusted sources, not internet rumors. Ultimately, I believe in speaking my mind clearly, respecting others' choices but standing firm on science and faith, without getting overly emotional, thanks to my low neuroticism.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion by integrating persona traits: age (80) implies life experience with health crises; gender (man) and sexual orientation (asexual) not heavily weighted but inform neutral perspective; occupation (retired) and city (New York) suggest urban, community-focused view; education (vocational diploma) and parents' education indicate practical, not highly academic, approach; marital status (married) and children (2) emphasize family and social responsibility; migration status (native-born American) and religious beliefs (Christianity) support pro-vaccine stance aligned with community care; psychological feature (curious) encourages inquiry but low openness (score 3) limits acceptance of new ideas; high extraversion (score 68) leads to outspoken, engaging discussion style; low agreeableness (score 14) results in direct, non-confrontational but firm communication; low neuroticism (score 7) ensures calm, reasoned tone; time on social media (several hours daily) implies exposure to debates but skepticism towards misinformation; hobbies (reading, hiking, painting) highlight desire for active, safe lifestyle post-vaccination. Opinion crafted as continuous prose, avoiding bullet points, focusing on managing discussions through personal narrative and practical advice, aligning with persona's background.
response_parsed.tone: Direct, experienced, and community-oriented, with a calm and firm demeanor, reflecting low agreeableness and high extraversion, infused with Christian values and practical wisdom from age and urban living.
